Permutation Test For ImmPort Pathway Occurrences

Source: R/ImmPort.R
PermutationTestForImmPortPathwayOccurrences.Rd

Tests whether ImmPort pathways are over-represented in a gene set relative to random draws from the ImmPort gene universe. For each pathway observed in the input set, the observed gene count is compared against a null distribution built by resampling the same number of genes from the full ImmPort gene space.

PermutationTestForImmPortPathwayOccurrences(
  geneSet,
  nPermutations = 1000,
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  seed = GetSeed()
)

Arguments

geneSet

A character vector of gene symbols (e.g., DE result genes). Case-insensitive.

nPermutations

Number of random resamples used to build the null distribution. Default is 1000.

pAdjustMethod

Multiple testing correction method passed to p.adjust. Default is "BH".

seed

Integer random seed for reproducibility. Default is GetSeed().

Value

A data.frame ordered by ascending PValue with columns:

  • Pathway: ImmPort pathway name.

  • ObservedCount: Number of input genes mapping to the pathway.

  • MeanNullCount: Mean count across all permutations.

  • PValue: Empirical one-tailed p-value (proportion of permutations where null count >= observed).

  • PValueAdj: Multiple-testing-adjusted p-value.

Contents
